stimulus: Music writers have called Celia Cruz the “queen of salsa,” an epithet that has appeared in countless retrospectives since the 1980s. Cruz herself resisted the queen ______blank">blank insisting that her career belonged to a tradition far larger than any one performer.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The conjunctive adverb "however" interrupts a single clause and must be set off with paired commas. The main clause is "Cruz herself resisted the queen label," and the participial phrase "insisting…performer" continues that clause.

Choice A is incorrect because it omits the closing comma after "however." Choice B is incorrect because a semicolon after "however" wrongly suggests an independent clause follows. Choice C is incorrect because a semicolon before "however" is used only when an independent clause follows; here, "insisting…" is a participial phrase, not an independent clause.
